notes: Inferred from 2023 betting plays — Forte to win/show favorite, Angel of Empire as the second-best, Two Phil's as the price flier.
analysis: One of the most wide-open races (5-6 horses) and one I don't have a clear lean to win. The best horses in this field will start mid-pack to dead last. Without a collective push on pace, look for whichever horse can start well, be around 5th at the first turn and make a move up to first into the second turn. Forte drawing an outside post will help him swing outside to make a move — main bet, will probably pay even odds to show. Angel of Empire is another horse that, if positioned well, could push around the final turn with a shot to win.
betting_plays:
Forte to Show (main bet)
H2H Angel of Empire over Tapit Trice (if offered in props)

Exactas
Boxing Kingsbarns, Mage, Angel of Empire, Forte, and Derma Sotogake (20 ways)

Trifectas
Boxing Mage, Forte, and Angel of Empire while adding Derma Sotogake and Kingsbarns up top (18 ways)
Adding Two Phil's underneath (30 ways)

Superfectas
Mixed two-horse keyed lines plus a wider underneath

horses.name: Forte
horses.note: very versatile, post will let him duck mid-pack but use outside late

horses.name: Angel of Empire
horses.note: closer who could break first if positioned well

horses.name: Tapit Trice
horses.note: very strong but stalking style won't fare in slower field

horses.name: Kingsbarns
horses.note: perfect post + pace plays in his favor; tricky pick

horses.name: Derma Sotogake
horses.note: Japanese eye-test pick; UAE Derby winner is 0-18 in the Derby though

horses.name: Mage
horses.note: only three races; getting better each time

horses.name: Two Phil's
horses.note: popular bet, but coming off a win against a weak field

horses.name: Confidence Game
horses.note: huge wild card — perfect post; 10-week layoff scares people

horses.name: Verifying
horses.note: fast horse but not the best

horses.name: Reincarnate
horses.note: will press pace, then die mid-race

horses.writeup:
3-1-0 of 5 races
Lost Wood Memorial to Lord Miles at the end
Was in control, was heavy favorite
Hasn’t beat many good horses
Same barn from Brad Cox in addition to Angel of Empire and Verifying

horses.final_take: Fast horse, but I don’t think he is the best horse. I like him to be near the front but fade at the end. Could surprise win
horses.writeup:
2-2-0 of 6 races
Either barely loses or is blown out
Got lost behind crowd in Rebel Stakes, didn’t navigate well, but once clear of horses he finished fast
Had a perfect trip at Blue Grass then barely lost to Tapit Trice
Looked fast here
Depends on the trip. I actually like this horse
Distance could be a concern at the end

horses.final_take: Two Phil’s will be a popular bet, but I don’t think he is anything special and is coming off a win against a bunch of dorks. Might still included underneath in some exotics. 8-1 is not worth the price for him.
horses.writeup:
Glorified version of Simplification from last year: extremely strong horse who very well could hit the board
Won on turf but was bred on dirt and has won at Churchill downs (wet)
Good speed rating
Could be good for a price
Not well known trainer, but is quietly good
One of most talked about horses about his price and potential, but also tossed out by many handicappers as not a top tier horse
Lost Risen Star behind Angel of Empire
Hasn’t looked overly impressive in film
Jockey has never ridden in the Derby

horses.final_take: Huge wild card here. Perfect post for this horse. He could be a horse to take a flier on to get a good payout. People will pass him up solely on his 10 week rest. Quietly passing on this horse but I don’t think he’s dead
horses.writeup:
3-1-2 of 7 races
Won Rebel Stakes barely over Red Route One
Navigated big field and came back to extend a big lead and win
Has won at Churchill over Rocket Can(’t)
Hasn’t raced in over 10 weeks (no horse has ever won after a 7 week layoff)
Better in rainy slop
Not too fast of a horse, big win may have been over easy field (recheck speed)
Bred for distance, yet also likes to get to the front 👀
Good final splits

horses.final_take: Very strong horse, will have low odds for a reason. However, his style of racing near back of the pack then closing will not bode well in this slower field. Will be tough getting around a lot of horses. Will play him in 3/4 spots in exotics.
horses.writeup:
4-0-1 lifetime
Won bluegrass over Verifying by a neck
Hasn’t blown anyone out
Likes to stalk the lead which bodes well for his post position
I like this horse to be in the conversation but not quite win
Include on trifecta tickets
Starts slow
** Extremely similar horse to Angel of Empire. Between the two I prefer Angel of Empire.

horses.final_take: Drew a perfect post, pace of race will play in his favor, but might have just beat bad horses in prior races making him seem better. Tricky pick here, but a real win contender
horses.writeup:

Other two wins against nobody

Unimpressive speed figures
Maybe because hasn’t been tested
Looked strong in Louisiana, but field sucked

horses.final_take: Will press pace to start the race and will inevitably die mid race and finish in back. 15th.

Can go the distance, good finisher
Good value horse
Looked like a boring easy win at Arkansas
Not overly impressive

No doubt can close, but unfortunately always starts in the back which may make his trip extra difficult in a slower paced derby. If pace ends up being quick or even average, AoE could definitely win.

horses.final_take: Safe play here, probably the best chance to win, but depends what his odds are prior to the race.
horses.writeup:

horses.final_take: Hurricanes will win the cup this year. This horse will not win the Derby.

horses.final_take: I will play him to win and up top in some exotics. Hopefully will go off higher than 7-1. Japan betters will hammer him and drive his payout down. Not using underneath in exotics because if he is in fact not the best, he could very well be a mid finishing horse. Feel like its win or bust.

If you liked Practical Move, then you should love this horse. He is a Japan horse (has lost close to Derma Sotogake and raced on a lower Japanese circuit than Derma) that came to California for the Santa Ana qualifier and almost rallied to beat Practical Move who just scratched, but people loved and was going to go off at around 10-1. Could be a good value play
